rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS REILLY et al VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER, A et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 19, 2007, LINE 6. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE FOURTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON THE UNDISPUTED FACTS ESTABLISHING NO LIABILITY BASED ON LISA M. V HENRY NEWHALL MEMORIAL HOSPITAL (1995) 12 CAL. TH 291. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED EVIDENCE THAT RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT; RATHER PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE AND THE INFERENCES THEREFROM CONSTITUTE SPECULATION THAT WHAT OCCURRED WAS AN ACCIDENTAL TOUCHING NOT SEXUAL BATTERY. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: NEIL ADAMS VS. CORT BUSINESS SERVICES CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 19, 2007, LINE 8. DEFENDANT DAWN KLIPPEL'S DEMURRER TO FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. rulings.Case Title: RICHARD L MILLER VS. BERNARD N WOLF et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 19, 2007, LINE 10. DEFENDANT BERNARD N. WOLF'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. GIVEN THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT REPRESENT PLAINTIFF DURING THE REMAND PROCEEDINGS, THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS RAN FROM THE DATE OF THE FIRST COURT OF APPEAL DECISION. ALTERNATIVELY, DEFENDANT'S REPRESENTATION WAS TERMINATED BY PLAINTIFF ON MARCH 20, 2006 AND THIS SUIT WAS NOT FILED UNTIL MARCH 27, 2007. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISGORGEMENT IS BASED UPON PLAINTIFF'S LEGAL MALPRACTICE CLAIM AND IS BARRED BY THE ONE YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. C.C.P. SECTION 340.6. FURTHER, IT IS UNLIKELY THAT PLAINTIFF CAN STATE A CLAIM BASED UPON DEFENDANT'S PREPARATION OF A STATEMENT OF DECISION AND THE ALLEGATIONS RELATED TO DEFENDANT'S HANDLING OF THE APPEAL LACK THE REQUISITE SPECIFICITY TO ALLEGE A LEGAL MALPRACTICE CLAIM. =(302/PJM/VC)
